Stack's Bowers Galleries (& Ponterio) - January 2026 NYINC Auction, Lot 40264 — THEODOSIUS II, A.D. 402-450. AR Miliarensis (4.31 gms), Constantinople Mint, ca. A.D. 408-420. NGC MS, Strike: 4/5 Surface: 5/5. Die Shift.

sales.description:
THEODOSIUS II, A.D. 402-450. AR Miliarensis (4.31 gms), Constantinople Mint, ca. A.D. 408-420. NGC MS, Strike: 4/5 Surface: 5/5. Die Shift.
RIC-370; RSC-20a. Obverse: Pearl-diademed, draped, and cuirassed bust left, seen from front; Reverse: Theodosius standing facing, raising hand and holding globus; star to left; CON. Though a bit of a die shift is noted on each side, this specimen presents exceptional eye appeal with iridescent color nearer the edges of each side.

sales.description:
ROMAN EMPIRE

Cornelia Supera, wife of Aemilian, 253.
Antoninianus 253, Constantinople. CORNEL SUPER AVG Diademed bust of Cornelia Supera right on crescent / VESTA Veiled and draped Vesta standing left, holding patera in right hand and transverse scepterin left hand. 3,37g. C. 5; RIC IV 30.
NGC Ch XF 4/5 4/5.
(6645988-003).
Extremely rare. A beautiful specimen of illustrious provenance.

Provenance: Merzbacher (2 November 1909), 2016.
